title: Register as a first-time voter
subtitle: If you just turned 18 (or about to)

intro.title: Becoming a voter is your right.
intro.body: We'll walk you through how to register, what you need, and what to expect. Takes about 8 minutes.

ageCheck.title: Have you turned 18?
ageCheck.body: You can register to vote on any of four qualifying dates: January 1, April 1, July 1, or October 1 of the year.

choices.label: Yes, I've turned 18
choices.sublabel: Or will turn 18 by the next qualifying date

tooYoung.title: You'll be eligible soon.
tooYoung.body:
Bookmark this journey. Once you turn 17, you can pre-register so your name appears in the roll the moment you turn 18.

India introduced 4 qualifying dates per year specifically so young voters don't lose any time.

documents.title: You'll need three things.
documents.body: Don't worry if you don't have them all right now — most are easy to arrange.

items.label: Age proof
items.hint: Aadhaar, Birth Certificate, 10th certificate, Passport, or PAN

items.label: Address proof
items.hint: Aadhaar, recent utility bill (last 3 months), bank passbook, or rent agreement

items.label: Recent passport-size photograph
items.hint: One color photo with white background

applyOnline.title: Apply online — it's free.
applyOnline.body: Form 6 is the official application for new voters. The portal is the fastest way. You can also visit your nearest BLO if you prefer offline.

afterSubmit.title: Your application begins a verification.
afterSubmit.body:
**A Booth Level Officer (BLO) will visit your home** to verify your address. This usually happens within 2–4 weeks.

Keep your documents ready. The BLO will confirm your details and approve your inclusion in the electoral roll.

trackStatus.title: You can check your application anytime.
trackStatus.body: Use your reference number on the same portal to see if your form is verified, accepted, or needs corrections.

complete.title: You're on your way to becoming a voter.
complete.summary: Once approved, you'll receive your EPIC (Voter ID card). You can then check your name at electoralsearch.eci.gov.in and find your polling booth.
